block_02: Every four years, American citizens elect the U.S. president, while investors all over the world try to figure out how the outcome might affect the market.
block_03: The US elections 2020 will take place in less than a week, on November 3. In this newsletter we’ll review the upcoming elections with a focus on its candidates and their key initiatives. Below is a comparative table of this year nominees, Donald J. Trump and Joe Biden:
block_06: His Jobs Act of 2017 has lowered corporate income taxes to 21%
block_07: Rolled back limits on carbon dioxide emissions from power plants and vehicles
block_08: Most recently, approved oil and gas drilling in Alaska
block_09: Imposed tariffs on goods from European Union
block_10: Strong and tough foreign policy against China
block_11:

block_12: Proposing a $2 trillion plan to fight climate change
block_13: His electric charging stations infrastructure will favor green technologies
block_15: Looking for closer economic ties with China
